title: Preventive Health & Longevity Habits
description: Reflect on sleep, movement, nutrition, and stress — a quick snapshot of habits that support long-term health.

questions.text: How would you rate your typical sleep (7 nights)?

options.label: Often under 6 hours or poor quality

questions.text: How much intentional movement do you get per week?
How would you describe your day-to-day eating pattern?

options.label: Mix of healthy and processed / irregular meals
Frequent ultra-processed food or skipped meals

questions.text: How is your stress level most days?
When did you last have preventive check-ups (e.g. blood pressure, labs, age-appropriate screening)?

bands.title: Strong foundation
bands.summary: Your habits support long-term health. A longevity-focused consultation can personalise screening and optimisation based on your goals and history.
recommendations: Keep a consistent sleep schedule where possible
Maintain movement you enjoy — consistency matters more than intensity
Discuss age-appropriate labs and screening with your doctor

bands.title: Room to grow
bands.summary: Several lifestyle areas could benefit from small, sustainable changes. Structured support often helps turn intention into lasting habits.
recommendations: Pick one habit to improve first (sleep, movement, or meals)
Track progress for 2–3 weeks before adding another change
Consider a preventive health consultation to set priorities

bands.title: Priority focus
bands.summary: Your answers suggest multiple areas where targeted lifestyle and medical support could meaningfully improve wellbeing and long-term risk.
recommendations: Book a longevity or general preventive consultation
Address sleep and stress with realistic, gradual goals
Screen for cardiometabolic risk with your clinician
